positive_description: Right-lateralized mid-face and cheek deformation. A subtle right-side cheek compression or hollowing, with mild adjustment to the right nasolabial fold. The right side of the mid-face tightens while the left remains relatively neutral.
negative_description: Right-lateralized mid-face relaxation. The right cheek fills slightly and the right nasolabial area softens. The right side eases while the left stays neutral.
symmetry_notes: Asymmetric — this is the right-sided member of the psi17/psi18 conjugate pair. Mirrors psi17 across the sagittal plane. The 3/4 views show right-side bias in the deformation.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Very subtle mid-frequency component. Safe at extended weights.
primary_face_region: right cheek, right mid-face, right nasolabial area
useful_for: bilateral mid-face expression (as conjugate pair with psi17)

interactions.psi17: CONJUGATE PAIR. psi18+psi17 at equal weights produces bilateral mid-face expression.
interactions.psi12: Same conjugate architecture — psi11/12 for mouth, psi14/15 for brows, psi17/18 for mid-face
interactions.psi3: psi3 drives symmetric mid-face expression; psi17/18 provides asymmetric control

prose: Psi18 is the right-handed mirror of psi17 — the second member of the psi17/psi18 conjugate pair. It lateralizes mid-face and cheek deformation to the right side. At positive values, the right cheek tightens and the right nasolabial area adjusts. At negative values, the right mid-face softens. Paired with psi17 at equal weight, the two produce bilateral mid-face expression. The deformations are very subtle at the tested weights, consistent with decreasing PCA variance at higher component indices.
